cue: a fixed stencil move applied uniformly on a board/cycle; reachability asked
note: all annihilate the stencil with a position-weighting/coloring to get a conserved quantity

cue: a 2-variable functional equation where substituting f(·) for a variable recombines terms
note: the substitution collapses the relation into one among consecutive iterates of f

cue: a for-all-n relation built from a running sum or sliding-window product
note: subtract or divide two index-shifted copies; the shared block cancels to a per-index relation

cue: more collected items than a finite state set (residues, parity-vectors) admits
note: more collected items than available states forces a state to repeat

cue: the objects share a common factor, or the extremal value is divisible
note: divide the common factor out to pass to a smaller or coprime equivalent instance (descent)

cue: a line tangent to a circle at a named point
note: tangent-chord angle equals the inscribed angle on that chord, and its converse

cue: two figures with parallel corresponding sides, or shared circumcenter and orthocenter
note: parallel correspondence forces a homothety (or translation); the task is to locate its center

cue: a selection under a spacing constraint, each pick forcing an exclusion
note: amortize each included pick against a forced skip to bound the selection size

cue: a process whose bounded monotone count targets its own extreme value
note: classify which moves keep it from changing, forcing it to reach the target or halt

cue: a point on a side's extension beyond a vertex, supplementing an apex angle
note: the supplement manufactures the second equal-angle pair needed for AA similarity

cue: coinciding upper and lower bounds, or k terms each ≥m summing to km
note: coinciding bounds force equality throughout the chain

cue: an optimizer of a weighted quantity admitting a one-step-perturbed admissible candidate
note: read the governing inequality straight off the admissible perturbation

cue: an inequality comparing an exponential term r^t against a linear quantity
note: a tangent-line / Bernoulli bound linearizes the exponential contribution

cue: two divisibility relations sharing a common left factor
note: subtract one from a multiple of the other to collapse onto a tighter single divisibility

cue: two circles meeting again, or two triangles sharing a vertex with proportional sides
note: that common point is the spiral-similarity center carrying one chord/triangle to the other

cue: a circle point equidistant from two others, or a bisector's second circumcircle meeting
note: such a point is an arc-midpoint / circumcenter, giving a perpendicular, antiparallel chord, or central=2*inscribed

cue: an equation forcing one side to a prime power, exponent unknown
note: extract the prime-exponent on each side and compare (LTE/Legendre downstream)

cue: constant difference or ratio of distances to two fixed points or lines
note: constant difference yields a conic with those foci; constant ratio yields an Apollonius circle / fixed line

cue: an intersection point that must also satisfy a separate positional property
note: introduce a phantom point carrying the wanted property and show it meets the defining condition, so they coincide

cue: three circles with pairwise similitude centers; a fourth center forced onto a line
note: all reduce a concurrency/tangency goal to collinearity of the pairwise homothety centers of a circle-triple (Monge)

cue: a point with equal power to two circles, or equal signed-segment products
note: all open by placing an equal-power point on a radical axis, yielding concyclic / coaxial / radical-center conclusions

cue: a sum times its reciprocal-sum, or two summands with controlled product
note: expanding the cross terms of (sum)(reciprocal-sum) regroups them into the controlled diagonal products

cue: a product of positive quantities with fixed sum, maximized or bounded
note: split the fixed sum into equal / complementary parts to extremize the product

cue: a per-n extremal construction with logarithmic (~log2 n) target size
note: build index-layers of geometrically doubling size (2-adic valuation / floor(log2)) so a monotone path or class meets at most one per layer

cue: a merge fusing two equal piles into one double pile; pile-count target
note: doubling-tournament ladder plus a reservoir pile; demoted from DOUBLING_CONSTRUCT because its trigger is a pile-merge op, not a log-bound layout

cue: a target represented with a prescribed number of distinct summands
note: pad to the required count with value-canceling (sign-symmetric) pairs that contribute zero to the total

cue: a prescribed multiset of pairwise differences realized by points on a line
note: place points as partial sums of powers of a root of a tailored recurrence so block-sums collapse to single powers

cue: a process closed under addition and coordinatewise maximum, reaching a target tuple
note: build spike/basis vectors from generators spanning {1,index,index^2}; folds in the bound-side closure invariant for the same problem

cue: a finite integer set where each pairwise difference divides both endpoints
note: a fixed gadget {0,6,8,9,12} scaled by the lcm of its differences gives an infinite family

cue: a self-similar multiset built from two half-size copies, constant per-block target
note: recursive split T_m = (T_{m-1}+1) union {1..m}, two copies concatenated

cue: a prescribed per-position shift realized by transposing complementary-length blocks of a strip
note: pieces sized n, n-1, ... paired with 1, 2, ... each summing to n, swap each pair

cue: every summary-quantity value realized from one template with a free scaling parameter
note: family (l, kl, kl, k^2 l) with l tuned to hit every non-squarefree total
